input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android when targeting .NET 10. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` (which worked in .NET 9) no longer works.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode was deprecated, and in .NET MAUI for .NET 10, they may have moved to using the newer `WindowInsets` API approach.

Let me provide the correct guidance:

In .NET 10 (and with Android API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` is deprecated. 

The recommended approach now is to use the Android `WindowInsets` API, which MAUI handles through `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` in newer versions—a built-in feature that automatically scrolls content when the keyboard appears—or by manually managing `WindowInsets` on Android. The older `UseWindowSoftInputModeAdjust` approach that worked in .NET 9 has been superseded by these newer methods.

For .NET 10 specifically, `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` is enabled by default and should handle keyboard avoidance automatically, though if it's not working, the user might need to verify it hasn't been disabled or wrap their content in a `ScrollView`. The modern Android approach favors `WindowInsets` APIs over the deprecated `SoftInputMode.AdjustResize`.
